title: Ike's Morning Digest
data_note: Built from publisher RSS metadata (titles + teaser text). `coverage_window_hours` is the default (40h); several feeds use a longer `rollingHours` (see `scripts/build-digest.mjs`) so rows align with what is still on each publisher’s listing pages. Commercial Observer keeps its retail category + hub supplement; TRD adds a New York–filtered global retail tag feed. PincusCo uses the live site feed only (`/category/retail/feed/` is stale). **CRE Daily** retail briefs come from `credaily.com/sectors/retail/feed/`. Open each link for the full article (subscriptions may apply in the browser).

section_overviews.commercial_observer:
Luxury Jewelry Brand David Yurman Takes 685 Fifth’s Entire 23K-SF Retail Condo: Luxury jewelry retailer David Yurman has leased the entire retail condominium at 685 Fifth Avenue , just one month after a $160 million loan on the property was transferred to special servicing.

Fred Astaire Dance Studios Leases First Park Slope Location: The international dance franchise Fred Astaire Dance Studios is waltzing into Brooklyn’s Park Slope neighborhood. The school’s new lease at 508 Fifth Avenue spans 2,500 square feet of ground-floor retail space previously occupied by shoe store Bobby Sportswear .

Cashmere Brand Kujten Picks Acadia’s 92 Greene Street for Second U.S. Location: French cashmere brand Kujten has leased a second Manhattan location less than a year after opening its first U.S. flagship along Madison Avenue.

Bistro Pardon My French Sets Up Shop at Jeff Sutton’s 373 Broome Street: Ooh la la. French-inspired bistro Pardon My French is coming to Manhattan’s SoHo neighborhood after signing a 1,000-square-foot lease at 373 Broome Street , a property owned in a joint venture between Jeff Sutton ’s Wharton Properties and Gorjian Real Estate Group, according to broker Meridian Capital Group .
section_overviews.cre_daily:
Blackstone Lends $125M Refi to Dallas Mixed-Use Complex: Harbor Group International landed a $125M refinancing from Blackstone for the Alesio Urban Center in Irving, Texas. The 908-unit mixed-use property includes 55,500 SF of retail and recently underwent a $4.5M value-add program.

Drive-Thru Traffic Slides, Challenging Net Lease Retail: Short-duration, drive-thru visits to quick-service restaurants fell 6.8% year-over-year in May per Placer.ai. Full-service restaurants and experiential dining gained momentum even as drive-thru traffic declined.

URW Acquires Full Control Of San Diego’s Westfield UTC Mall: Unibail-Rodamco-Westfield is acquiring the remaining half of Westfield UTC for $705M. The 1M SF San Diego mall is set for further luxury expansion, with new brands arriving in 2026.

US Core Retail Sales Hit Record as Shoppers Shift Online: Core retail sales increased 0.5% month-over-month and 5.6% year-over-year in May, reflecting resilient consumer spending. Online and discount retailers saw record sales, while restaurant and electronics spending slowed as households cut back on discretionary purchases.
section_overviews.new_york_post:
Billionaire tech CEO latest to ditch California with $18M sale of sprawling ranch: Former eBay CEO Meg Whitman has unloaded her 1,530-acre Fall River Mills ranch for $17.9M. In 2025, she and her husband sold a penthouse atop a downtown Sacramento hotel for nearly $2.4 million.

Dallas OKs incentive package for $1.3B Morgan Stanley office tower — and far-left election wins in NYC could spark more businesses to leave: Officials rubber-stamped the deal that would grant the New York-based bank a special economic grant and up to a decade of business personal property tax abatements.

The nation’s most competitive housing market where 58% of homes sell above ask is a surprising New Jersey city: In this city, nearly three in five homes sold above ask in May -- the highest share of the nation's 50 most populous metros.

Booming town of Silverwood where homes are $400K and buyers have to sign ‘kindness’ waiver: The 9,000-acre project first grabbed headlines for its relatively affordable home prices — starting in the low $400,000s.
section_overviews.pincusco:
Getzel Neustadt pays $3.5M for retail condo in Lower East Side: Getzel Neustadt through the entity 150 Rivington Retail LLC, paid $3.5 million to the entity.

CW Realty Group signs 99-year lease valued at $9.7M with Kimco for LIC dev site: CW Realty Group through the entity 44 Drive GL LLC as tenant signed a ground.

Oleksandr Nad signs $13M construction loan with Colorado Federal for 18-unit project in Two Bridges: Oleksandr Nad through the entity 83 Madison Holdings LLC as borrower signed a new construction.

Gora Group pays $16.8M to Treeline Companies for office in Brooklyn Heights: Gora Group through the entity 188 Montague LLC paid $16.8 million to Treeline Companies through.
section_overviews.the_real_deal:
NYC’s top construction permits: Week ending June 19, 2026: New Building: The largest new building application filed last week was in Crotona Park East, where Luce Popaj plans to build two residential buildings at 1489 and 1495 Boston Road, spanning a combined 73,689 square feet. The buildings will house a total of 127 units and stand nine and 12 stories tall.

Brooklyn developer scores financing on one-time David Werner conversion target: David Werner’s loss is Hershy Silberstein’s gain. The Blue Sky Builders honcho landed $102 million in construction financing for the office-to-residential conversion of 311 West 43rd Street in Hell’s Kitchen, the Commercial Observer reported.

Scott Rechler made it to the other side of the downturn. At what cost?: When the one-two punch of Covid and higher interest rates cratered the country’s office market, Scott Rechler was more candid about the seismic problems than most large New York City landlords.

NYC’s top deals: Phil Donahue’s UES penthouse sells for $34M: There were 203 transactions totaling $412 million filed in New York City records in the 24 hours before 4 p.m. on Wednesday, June 24.

items.headline: Luxury Jewelry Brand David Yurman Takes 685 Fifth’s Entire 23K-SF Retail Condo
items.summary: Luxury jewelry retailer David Yurman has leased the entire retail condominium at 685 Fifth Avenue , just one month after a $160 million loan on the property was transferred to special servicing. The jewelry brand will open a new 23,000-square-foot David Yurman flagship store at the base of the 29-story Midtown building, according to landlord GGP , the retail subsidiary of Brookfield Corporation .…

items.headline: Fred Astaire Dance Studios Leases First Park Slope Location
items.summary: The international dance franchise Fred Astaire Dance Studios is waltzing into Brooklyn’s Park Slope neighborhood. The school’s new lease at 508 Fifth Avenue spans 2,500 square feet of ground-floor retail space previously occupied by shoe store Bobby Sportswear . The 10-year deal’s asking rent was $72 per square foot. The lease is the New York dance studio franchise’s sixth deal completed by Lee & Associates NYC ‘s Claude Kolb .…

items.headline: Cashmere Brand Kujten Picks Acadia’s 92 Greene Street for Second U.S. Location
items.summary: French cashmere brand Kujten has leased a second Manhattan location less than a year after opening its first U.S. flagship along Madison Avenue. The upcoming store at Acadia Realty Trust ’s 92 Greene Street , a midblock address bordered by Spring and Prince streets, sits in the heart of SoHo’s bustling shopping corridor. Kujten signed a long-term, 2,567-square-foot lease, with 1,531 square feet on the ground floor and 1,036 square feet on the lower level, according to landlord broker Newmark .…

items.headline: Bistro Pardon My French Sets Up Shop at Jeff Sutton’s 373 Broome Street
items.summary: Ooh la la. French-inspired bistro Pardon My French is coming to Manhattan’s SoHo neighborhood after signing a 1,000-square-foot lease at 373 Broome Street , a property owned in a joint venture between Jeff Sutton ’s Wharton Properties and Gorjian Real Estate Group, according to broker Meridian Capital Group . The asking rent for the ground-floor retail space was $225 per square foot. The length of the lease, which was signed at the end of April, was not disclosed.…

items.headline: Blackstone Lends $125M Refi to Dallas Mixed-Use Complex
items.summary: Harbor Group International landed a $125M refinancing from Blackstone for the Alesio Urban Center in Irving, Texas. The 908-unit mixed-use property includes 55,500 SF of retail and recently underwent a $4.5M value-add program. The deal underscores lender appetite for stable, large-scale multifamily assets in the Dallas market despite national debt market volatility.…

items.headline: Drive-Thru Traffic Slides, Challenging Net Lease Retail
items.summary: Short-duration, drive-thru visits to quick-service restaurants fell 6.8% year-over-year in May per Placer.ai. Full-service restaurants and experiential dining gained momentum even as drive-thru traffic declined. CRE investors should reexamine assumptions about outparcel and net lease assets anchored by pure convenience demand.…

items.headline: URW Acquires Full Control Of San Diego’s Westfield UTC Mall
items.summary: Unibail-Rodamco-Westfield is acquiring the remaining half of Westfield UTC for $705M. The 1M SF San Diego mall is set for further luxury expansion, with new brands arriving in 2026. The deal reinforces URW’s pivot to hold top-performing US retail assets, defying the trend of mall selloffs. Key Takeaways Luxury Retail’s Resilience In San Diego Unibail-Rodamco-Westfield (URW) has agreed to buy out its joint venture partner’s 50% stake in San Diego’s Westfield UTC for $705M in cash and shares, according to Bisnow.…

items.headline: US Core Retail Sales Hit Record as Shoppers Shift Online
items.summary: Core retail sales increased 0.5% month-over-month and 5.6% year-over-year in May, reflecting resilient consumer spending. Online and discount retailers saw record sales, while restaurant and electronics spending slowed as households cut back on discretionary purchases. Absorption in retail real estate turned positive in Q2 2026, driven by demand for big-box and value-focused tenants.…

items.headline: Toys and Collectibles Fuel Booming US Retail Foot Traffic
items.summary: Toys and collectibles are rapidly boosting foot traffic and sales for US retailers, drawing demand from Gen Z, Millennials, and older buyers alike. Brands employ scarcity tactics and viral social trends, while global companies like Pop Mart and Miniso expand their US retail footprints to capitalize on the craze. The collectibles surge is reshaping retail leasing dynamics—offering landlords, developers, and brokers new opportunities for high-frequency, intent-driven tenants.…

items.headline: Workplace Safety Platform Intenseye Takes 9K SF at 220 West 19th Street
items.summary: Intenseye , an artificial intelligence-powered workplace safety platform, has signed a full-floor, 9,485-square-foot lease at Red Circle NY ’s 220 West 19th Street in Manhattan’s Chelsea neighborhood, according to landlord broker Avison Young . The security firm’s deal on the entire fourth floor was one of two new leases totaling 13,264 square feet at the 12-story office building between Seventh and Eighth avenues.…

items.headline: NYC’s top construction permits: Week ending June 19, 2026
items.summary: New Building: The largest new building application filed last week was in Crotona Park East, where Luce Popaj plans to build two residential buildings at 1489 and 1495 Boston Road, spanning a combined 73,689 square feet. The buildings will house a total of 127 units and stand nine and 12 stories tall. Nikolai Katz is the architect.…

items.headline: US Small Business Tenants Face Profit Pressures in 2026
items.summary: Small business tenants are experiencing falling profitability, with a 1.3% year-over-year drop per Bank of America Institute’s May 2026 data. The war in Iran and surging energy costs have driven up construction and operating expenses, challenging lease renewal and expansion plans. CRE landlords could soon see higher vacancy and default rates from their largest tenant segment as financial stress accumulates.…

items.headline: TA Realty Buys Two Staten Island Retail Buildings for $79M
items.summary: Boston-based real estate investment firm TA Realty has purchased two buildings within Staten Island’s Tysens Park Shopping Center , according to property records made public Monday. The James Raisides -led TA Realty purchased the properties at 2712 Hylan Boulevard and 2754 Hylan Boulevard in New Dorp Beach, Staten Island, for a total of $79.2 million from the Bregman Organization , a deed transfer in property records indicates .…

items.headline: French Cafe L’Ami Pierre to Open at Midtown’s 400 Madison Avenue
items.summary: A French cafe by acclaimed restaurateur Pierre-Antoine Raberin is opening a second location in Midtown, Commercial Observer has learned. Raberin has signed a 2,400-square-foot lease at 400 Madison Avenue for the second outpost of L’Ami Pierre , according to tenant broker Judson CRE . The deal follows the exit of Raberin’s partner, Michelin -starred chef Eric Ripert , in May 2023 .…

items.headline: Brooklyn developer scores financing on one-time David Werner conversion target
items.summary: David Werner’s loss is Hershy Silberstein’s gain. The Blue Sky Builders honcho landed $102 million in construction financing for the office-to-residential conversion of 311 West 43rd Street in Hell’s Kitchen, the Commercial Observer reported. S3 Capital provided the debt for the redevelopment of the Press Building. Silberstein’s involvement in the project wasn’t previously disclosed. Instead, it appeared that one of New York City’s conversion kings, David Werner, would be spearheading the redevelopment.…

items.headline: Scott Rechler made it to the other side of the downturn. At what cost?
items.summary: When the one-two punch of Covid and higher interest rates cratered the country’s office market, Scott Rechler was more candid about the seismic problems than most large New York City landlords. In 2023, the 58-year-old CEO publicized that RXR, his commercial property firm, had embarked on an initiative called “Project Kodak” that would separate the viable “digital” office buildings in its mega-portfolio from the film-like properties that were now suddenly obsolete. Three years later, that clean and…

items.summary: Former eBay CEO Meg Whitman has unloaded her 1,530-acre Fall River Mills ranch for $17.9M. In 2025, she and her husband sold a penthouse atop a downtown Sacramento hotel for nearly $2.4 million.

items.headline: NYC’s top deals: Phil Donahue’s UES penthouse sells for $34M
items.summary: There were 203 transactions totaling $412 million filed in New York City records in the 24 hours before 4 p.m. on Wednesday, June 24. Commercial: The biggest commercial sale to hit records was in Whitestone at 157-15 19th Avenue for about $58.18 million. The sale included the 47,000 square foot building and the adjacent parking lot. The seller was Clearview Land LLC, linked to the Grand Healthcare System. The buyer was a group of companies tied to Mark Friedman.…

items.headline: Phil Donahue, Marlo Thomas’ longtime Upper East Side co-op sold for $34M
items.summary: A trust tied to late TV host Phil Donahue and his wife Marlo Thomas found a buyer for their longtime Upper East Side co-op. The unit at 875 Fifth Avenue sold for $33.5 million, according to public records. The buyer’s identity was shielded by a trust, with attorneys Jonathan Moses and Kevin Schwartz of Wachtell, Lipton, Rosen & Katz listed as trustees. The duplex penthouse unit appears to have traded in an off-market deal.…

items.headline: The primary winners poised to shake up real estate
items.summary: A new wave of politicians is coming to Albany. The fight to represent New York in the state Senate and Assembly served as a fraught battleground of left-leaning candidates facing off against incumbents. Their wins — particularly those endorsed by Mayor Zohran Mamdani and the Democratic Socialists of America (as well as the Working Families Party) — stand to have an outsized impact on influencing city and state policy on the real estate industry and beyond. While many candidates won by touting progressive…

items.headline: NYC landlords see red as anti-real estate DSA candidates move closer to Congress: ‘Sets us up for failure’
items.summary: Claire Valdez and Darializa Avila Chevalier believe in seizure of property from alleged slumlords.
